response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a capable polyglot web and backend engineer with genuine strengths in distributed systems and testing culture, but she is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying gap is C++: the job requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet C++ does not appear in her skills section at all, and her only verifiable exposure is roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015. Her career trajectory is firmly in the TypeScript/Node.js/web ecosystem, with no evidence of cloud compute platform engineering, hardware-adjacent infrastructure, or formal technical leadership of engineering teams.
strengths: Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, processing millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency Dlang services.
Strong testing and software-craftsmanship ethos evidenced by open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), TDD practice, and community workshops on testing.
Broad polyglot experience across many stacks and environments (embedded, mobile, web, DevOps) showing adaptability and breadth of systems thinking.
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent — not listed in skills, and verifiable use is limited to ~6 months in 2014–2015, versus the 8-year minimum requirement.
No cloud compute platform or infrastructure engineering experience at enterprise scale; background is predominantly web application and backend service development.
No formal technical leadership or engineering management history; staff-level titles reflect individual contributor seniority, not team or program leadership.
